Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's economic shocks are derailing Britain's building plans
Finance & Economy.detail: According to Phillip Inman in The Guardian, Trump's economic policies are negatively impacting Britain's building sector, creating uncertainty and instability.

Finance & Economy.headline: High oil prices knock down stocks and erase Wall Street's hopes for a cut to interest rates
Finance & Economy.detail: Another climb for oil prices shook stock markets, as hopes collapsed for a possible cut to interest rates this year by the Federal Reserve, according to AP News.

Finance & Economy.headline: Ripple 2026 Survey Flags Stablecoin Push in TradFi
Finance & Economy.detail: Ripple's 2026 digital asset survey indicates that stablecoins are gaining traction in traditional finance, with 74% of respondents believing they can boost cash-flow efficiency.

Finance & Economy.headline: $4 petrol is coming, but be cautious of data showing it's already here
Finance & Economy.detail: According to RNZ News, it is likely that 91 octane petrol will hit $4 a litre soon in New Zealand, but caution is advised regarding Gaspy data indicating it has already reached that level.

Finance & Economy.headline: How has the Iran war impacted UK mortgages?
Finance & Economy.detail: ITV News reports that the war in Iran has sent shockwaves through the global economy, causing oil prices to surge and impacting financial markets, including UK mortgage rates, which have risen.

Technology.headline: AI Glasses Shift Into Momentum Mode, Shipments Grow 322% in 2025
Technology.detail: AI glasses shipments soared 322% in 2025 to 8.7 million units as advances in multimodal AI and lighter wearable design gain traction.

Technology.headline: Major Publisher Drops Horror Novel 'Shy Girl' Amid AI Allegations
Technology.detail: The publisher pulled 'Shy Girl' from Amazon and its website after allegations that it was written with the help of AI; the novel sold just under 2,000 copies in the UK and has been reviewed almost 5,000 times on Goodreads.

Technology.headline: Nintendo Switch 2 Could Soon Let Users Replace Their Own Batteries
Technology.detail: The new feature will be rolled out gradually for the Switch 2's detachable Joy-Con controllers sold in the EU, Nikkei Asia reports, ahead of the bloc's new rules coming into force.

Technology.headline: Less Copilot, More Reliability: Microsoft Eyes 'Quality' In New Windows 11 Updates
Technology.detail: Microsoft is reducing unnecessary Copilot entry points in new Windows 11 updates, focusing on improving the overall quality and reliability of the operating system.

Technology.headline: Court Finds Musk Misled Investors When He Bought Twitter
Technology.detail: A California court found that Elon Musk misled investors with a series of tweets claiming that 20% of Twitter consisted of bots and spam accounts, impacting the platform's share price, following a class-action lawsuit.

Health.headline: Lung surgeries not overused: ministry
Health.detail: The Ministry of Health and Welfare in Taiwan stated that lobectomy numbers have not significantly increased, addressing concerns that the government's lung cancer screening program has led to overdiagnosis. Data indicates a small rise in lung-related resection surgeries, but the number of lobectomy cases remains relatively stable.

Health.headline: Experts consider expanding meningitis vaccine eligibility after Kent outbreak
Health.detail: Following a meningitis outbreak in Kent, experts are considering expanding the eligibility for the meningitis vaccine. The UKHSA reported a slowdown in cases, suggesting the outbreak may have peaked.

Health.headline: Leo Horoscope Today, March 22, 2026: Health remains fine, but the unsettled mind needs care
Health.detail: According to astrological forecasts for Leos, overall health is expected to be fine, but it's important to pay attention to mental well-being and address any anxieties. The forecast encourages Leos to focus on maintaining a balanced state of mind.

Health.headline: The Saturday read. 'Something I've never felt since Covid. It was scarier': the shock and pain of Kent's meningitis outbreak.
Health.detail: A detailed account describes the experiences and impact of the recent meningitis outbreak in Kent, highlighting the fear and challenges faced by those affected. The article provides a personal perspective on the outbreak's severity and its impact on the community.

Health.headline: Global Health NOW: Home
Health.detail: Global Health NOW discusses the struggle to protect women in a warming world, noting that climate adaptation work for grassroots nonprofits often ends prematurely. They also cover the movement to phase out mercury in dental fillings and India's funding failure in rare genetic disease care.

Sports.headline: Altercation late in first half leads to 4 ejected from Thunder-Wizards
Sports.detail: A heated altercation during the first half resulted in the ejection of four players from the Thunder-Wizards game.

Sports.headline: Wizards guard Trae Young is out with multiple injuries, no timetable for return
Sports.detail: Trae Young, a guard for the Wizards, is sidelined due to multiple injuries, and there is no estimated return date.

Sports.headline: NBA fines Magic guard Jalen Suggs $25,000 for throwing mouthpiece
Sports.detail: Jalen Suggs, a guard for the Magic, has been fined $25,000 by the NBA for throwing his mouthpiece.

Sports.headline: Pirates reassign heralded prospect Konnor Griffin to minor league camp
Sports.detail: Konnor Griffin, a highly regarded prospect, has been reassigned to minor league camp by the Pirates after batting .184 in Grapefruit League action.

Sports.headline: Olympic champion swimmer Cameron McEvoy breaks the 50-meter freestyle world record
Sports.detail: Cameron McEvoy, an Olympic champion swimmer, has broken the world record in the 50-meter freestyle.

Entertainment.headline: Chuck Norris dies at age 86
Entertainment.detail: Action star Chuck Norris has died at age 86, his family announced Friday. He was known for his martial arts skills and film roles.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Frankie Paul Temporarily Loses Custody of Son With Dakota Mortenson After Brutal Fight Video
Entertainment.detail: Taylor Frankie Paul temporarily loses custody of her son with Dakota Mortenson after he was granted a protective order on Friday. The video of the fight surfaced and shows stools being thrown.

Entertainment.headline: British Reality TV Star Jordan Wright, 33, Found Dead in Thailand Drainage Ditch
Entertainment.detail: British reality TV star Jordan Wright was found dead in a drainage ditch in Thailand on Saturday. The 33-year-old's cause of death is currently under investigation.

Entertainment.headline: Amy Madigan wins Oscar for best supporting actress in 'Weapons,' 40 years after last nomination
Entertainment.detail: Amy Madigan won the Oscar for best supporting actress for her role in 'Weapons'. This marks her first Oscar win after 40 years since her last nomination.

Entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 screening in Canada goes chaotic; authorities step in following damage- watch viral clip
Entertainment.detail: The movie screens of Dhurandhar 2 in Montreal, Canada were sabotaged. Two theaters reported screen tears during the screening of the movie.

Crime & Justice.headline: Justice Department Sues Harvard University for Antisemitism
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department has filed a lawsuit against Harvard University alleging antisemitism.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former Bank CEO Pleads Guilty to Multimillion-Dollar Wire Fraud Conspiracy and Venezuela Sanctions Evasion Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: A former bank CEO has pleaded guilty to charges related to a multimillion-dollar wire fraud conspiracy and evading Venezuela sanctions.

Crime & Justice.headline: Five More Plead Guilty in Minnesota Feeding Our Future Fraud Scheme
Crime & Justice.detail: Five additional individuals have pleaded guilty in connection to the Feeding Our Future fraud scheme in Minnesota.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man accused of shooting father in face with crossbow captured after snowy manhunt
Crime & Justice.detail: A West Virginia man was arrested after allegedly shooting his father in the face with a crossbow and leading police on a snowy multicounty manhunt.

Crime & Justice.headline: Wife of Joseph Duggar charged with child endangerment after his arrest
Crime & Justice.detail: Kendra Duggar was charged with multiple misdemeanors a day after husband Joseph Duggar's arrest.

Education.headline: University Autonomy and Academic Freedom: ‘Unusually Steep’ Decline in U.S. University Autonomy
Education.detail: A recent index reveals an 'unusually steep' decline in U.S. university autonomy, suggesting that universities are facing increasing external pressures and constraints on their ability to govern themselves and make independent decisions. The factors contributing to this decline and its potential impact on academic freedom and institutional integrity warrant further investigation.

Education.headline: TDSB fighting city decision that protects school lands from redevelopment
Education.detail: The Toronto District School Board is actively fighting a city decision that would have protected school lands from redevelopment, raising concerns among community members about the potential sale of school properties to developers. The Ford government has taken over eight school boards, including the TDSB, giving the Education Minister more control over the sites and the power to decide which properties are no longer needed for students.

Education.headline: Schools Named for César Chavez Face Renaming Debates After Assault Allegations
Education.detail: Dozens of schools named for the labor leader César Chavez are weighing how to respond to new allegations. Schools around the country are weighing how to respond to new allegations about the labor leader.

Education.headline: Ottawa to cut science, tourism and foreign aid as government restructures spending.
Education.detail: The Canadian federal government plans to cut science, tourism, and foreign aid programs as part of a restructuring of spending. This decision may have implications for educational and research initiatives that rely on government funding.

Education.headline: Arts education in 'tremendous' demand across Asia
Education.detail: Arts education is experiencing a surge in demand throughout Asia, according to the LASALLE chief. This reflects a growing recognition of the importance of creative skills and cultural understanding in the region.

Lifestyle.headline: I lost my love of cooking after 12 years as a chef. Moving to a pig farm restored it.
Lifestyle.detail: A chef regains their passion for cooking after transitioning to working on a pig farm, highlighting the impact of environment on one's work.

Lifestyle.headline: On a Norwegian ferry cruise, scenery, camaraderie and a king crab feast.
Lifestyle.detail: Havila Voyages' coastal ferry sailings offer an eco-friendly, slow-travel alternative to traditional cruises, focusing on scenery and local experiences.

Lifestyle.headline: Miss Manners: Co-worker's comments about 'toxins' in food is offensive.
Lifestyle.detail: An advice column addresses the issue of a co-worker making offensive comments about 'toxins' in food, offering guidance on how to handle the situation.

Lifestyle.headline: Finland is picking travellers for a 7-day 'happiness retreat'—here's your chance to visit the world's happiest country for free.
Lifestyle.detail: Finland is offering a free 7-day 'happiness retreat' to selected travelers, providing an opportunity to experience the lifestyle and culture of the world's happiest country.

Lifestyle.headline: Why Women In Their 30s And 40s Need Strength Training
Lifestyle.detail: An article discusses the importance of strength training for women in their 30s and 40s, highlighting the health and lifestyle benefits.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A lifeline for PUV drivers
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing support for Public Utility Vehicle (PUV) drivers.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on meningitis in Kent: we must not take public health systems for granted
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial emphasizes the importance of maintaining strong public health systems, referencing a meningitis outbreak in Kent.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on anonymity in art: the 'unmasking' of Banksy and Ferrante should stop
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial argues against the revealing of anonymous artists like Banksy and Ferrante.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Rivian and the danger of building a business on government largesse
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board discusses Rivian and the risk of relying on government funding for business sustainability, noting the evolving EV market.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Remember the metaverse?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial reflecting on the current state and future prospects of the metaverse.

World.headline: Trump Threatens to 'Obliterate' Iran Power Plants Over Hormuz, Sets Deadline
World.detail: President Trump threatened to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened within 48 hours. The threat comes amidst ongoing tensions and military actions in the region.

World.headline: Iran Hits Israeli Town Housing Nuclear Facility in Retaliation for Natanz Strike
World.detail: Iran launched missile strikes on an Israeli town with a nuclear facility in response to a strike on Iran's Natanz nuclear site. Almost 100 people were reported wounded in the Iranian missile strikes on southern Israel.

World.headline: WHO says attack on Sudan hospital killed 64, including 13 children
World.detail: An attack on a hospital in Sudan resulted in the deaths of 64 people, including 13 children, according to the World Health Organization. The attack targeted Al-Daein Teaching Hospital in East Darfur state, western Sudan.

World.headline: EU urges members to start storing winter gas as Iran war causes price surge
World.detail: The European Union is urging its member states to begin storing gas for the winter due to a surge in prices caused by the war involving Iran. The conflict has led to volatile gas prices that could impact EU storage capacity.

World.headline: Israel says attacks against Iran will 'increase significantly' next week
World.detail: Israeli Defense Minister stated that the intensity of strikes on Iran's government and infrastructure by the United States and Israel would increase significantly in the coming week. The minister emphasized that the war would continue until all its goals are achieved.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt caps exam subjects, orders refunds for excess fees
Zimbabwe.detail: Candidates sitting for Zimbabwe School Examinations Council (Zimsec) Ordinary and Advanced Level examinations will now be restricted in the number of subjects, and the government has ordered refunds for excess fees paid.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim evacuates nationals from Iran, monitors Middle East
Zimbabwe.detail: The Government has completed the evacuation of Zimbabwean students and non-essential embassy staff from Iran, as the situation in the Middle East is being closely monitored.

Zimbabwe.headline: Nation prepares for solemn triple burial of national heroes
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa will preside over the burial of three national heroes: Major-General (Retired) Herbert Chingono.

Zimbabwe.headline: Fuel relief on the horizon . . . Govt moves to ease pain at the pump
Zimbabwe.detail: Consumers could soon get relief from rising fuel prices after President Mnangagwa established a high-level inter-ministerial committee.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare nurses demonstrate over low pay after fuel price surge
Zimbabwe.detail: Nurses at Sally Mugabe Central Hospital protested low salaries that they say no longer cover basic living costs after a sharp rise in fuel prices, with nurses resolving to stop going for night duty until the allowance was reviewed.
